title: Ordering, Single Active Consumer, and Partitioning · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for ordering, single active consumer, and partitioning and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a keyed order and failover state machine in Go. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use single active consumer when one active delivery stream is required and account for failover, prefetch, recovery, and throughput limits; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Ordering Scope Contract · changed case · Quiz Ordering Scope Contract
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 1123 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce keyed sequence progression and single-active failover limits; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. At RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Ordering Throughput Tradeoff, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 9218 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce keyed sequence progression and single-active failover limits; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Ordering Throughput Tradeoff, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Strict global order can scale horizontally without coordination cost. In Go, one Go receive channel guarantees global business-event order.
